title: Koyamasia curtisii

The health benefits of Koyamasia curtisii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Koyamasia curtisii include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Koyamasia curtisii include decoctions and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Koyamasia curtisii is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae and is native to Borneo. It is a type of climbing plant, and its classification further places it in the order Malpighiales. This order is part of the larger class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants. The class Magnoliopsida is in turn part of the division Magnoliophyta, which comprises flowering plants.
